column4: Now these are the {{c1::ordinances}} which thou shalt set before them.
Now these are the ordinances which thou shalt set {{c1::before}} them.
If thou buy a {{c1::Hebrew}} servant, six years he shall serve; and in the seventh he shall go out free for nothing.
If thou buy a Hebrew {{c1::servant}}, six years he shall serve; and in the seventh he shall go out free for nothing.
If he come in by {{c1::himself}}, he shall go out by himself; if he be married, then his wife shall go out with him.
If he come in by himself, he shall go out by himself; if he be {{c1::married}}, then his wife shall go out with him.
If his {{c1::master}} give him a wife, and she bear him sons or daughters; the wife and her children shall be her master’s, and he shall go out by himself.
If his master give him a wife, and she bear him sons or {{c1::daughters}}; the wife and her children shall be her master’s, and he shall go out by himself.
But if the servant shall plainly say: {{c1::I}} love my master, my wife, and my children; I will not go out free;
But if the {{c1::servant}} shall plainly say: I love my master, my wife, and my children; I will not go out free;
then his {{c1::master}} shall bring him unto God, and shall bring him to the door, or unto the door-post; and his master shall bore his ear through with an awl; and he shall serve him for ever.
then his master shall bring him unto God, and shall bring him to the door, or unto the door-post; and his master shall bore his ear {{c1::through}} with an awl; and he shall serve him for ever.
And if a man sell his {{c1::daughter}} to be a maid-servant, she shall not go out as the men-servants do.
And if a man sell his daughter to be a maid-{{c1::servant}}, she shall not go out as the men-servants do.

If she {{c1::please}} not her master, who hath espoused her to himself, then shall he let her be redeemed; to sell her unto a foreign people he shall have no power, seeing he hath dealt deceitfully with her.

If she please not her {{c1::master}}, who hath espoused her to himself, then shall he let her be redeemed; to sell her unto a foreign people he shall have no power, seeing he hath dealt deceitfully with her.
And if he {{c1::espouse}} her unto his son, he shall deal with her after the manner of daughters.
And if he espouse her unto his son, he shall deal with her after the {{c1::manner}} of daughters.
If he take him {{c1::another}} wife, her food, her raiment, and her conjugal rights, shall he not diminish.
If he take him another wife, her food, her {{c1::raiment}}, and her conjugal rights, shall he not diminish.
And if he do not these three unto her, then shall she go out for {{c1::nothing}}, without money.
And if he do not these three unto her, then shall she go out for nothing, {{c1::without}} money.
He that {{c1::smiteth}} a man, so that he dieth, shall surely be put to death.
He that smiteth a man, so that he dieth, shall {{c1::surely}} be put to death.
And if a man lie not in wait, but God cause it to come to hand; then {{c1::I}} will appoint thee a place whither he may flee.
And if a man lie not in wait, but God cause it to come to hand; then I will {{c1::appoint}} thee a place whither he may flee.
And if a man come presumptuously upon his neighbour, to slay him with guile; thou shalt take him from {{c1::Mine}} altar, that he may die.
And if a man come {{c1::presumptuously}} upon his neighbour, to slay him with guile; thou shalt take him from Mine altar, that he may die.
And he that {{c1::smiteth}} his father, or his mother, shall be surely put to death. .
And he that smiteth his {{c1::father}}, or his mother, shall be surely put to death. .
And he that {{c1::stealeth}} a man, and selleth him, or if he be found in his hand, he shall surely be put to death.
And he that stealeth a man, and {{c1::selleth}} him, or if he be found in his hand, he shall surely be put to death.
And he that {{c1::curseth}} his father or his mother, shall surely be put to death.
And he that curseth his {{c1::father}} or his mother, shall surely be put to death.
And if men {{c1::contend}}, and one smite the other with a stone, or with his fist, and he die not, but keep his bed;
if he rise again, and walk {{c1::abroad}} upon his staff, then shall he that smote him be quit; only he shall pay for the loss of his time, and shall cause him to be thoroughly healed.
if he rise again, and walk abroad upon his staff, then shall he that smote him be quit; only he shall pay for the loss of his time, and shall cause him to be {{c1::thoroughly}} healed.
And if a man smite his {{c1::bondman}}, or his bondwoman, with a rod, and he die under his hand, he shall surely be punished.
And if a man smite his bondman, or his {{c1::bondwoman}}, with a rod, and he die under his hand, he shall surely be punished.
{{c1::Notwithstanding}} if he continue a day or two, he shall not be punished; for he is his money.
Notwithstanding if he {{c1::continue}} a day or two, he shall not be punished; for he is his money.

And if men {{c1::strive}} together, and hurt a woman with child, so that her fruit depart, and yet no harm follow, he shall be surely fined, according as the woman’s husband shall lay upon him; and he shall pay as the judges determine.

And if men strive {{c1::together}}, and hurt a woman with child, so that her fruit depart, and yet no harm follow, he shall be surely fined, according as the woman’s husband shall lay upon him; and he shall pay as the judges determine.
But if any harm {{c1::follow}}, then thou shalt give life for life,
{{c1::burning}} for burning, wound for wound, stripe for stripe.
burning for burning, wound for wound, {{c1::stripe}} for stripe.
And if a man smite the eye of his {{c1::bondman}}, or the eye of his bondwoman, and destroy it, he shall let him go free for his eye’s sake.
And if a man smite the eye of his bondman, or the eye of his {{c1::bondwoman}}, and destroy it, he shall let him go free for his eye’s sake.
And if he smite out his {{c1::bondman’s}} tooth, or his bondwoman’s tooth, he shall let him go free for his tooth’s sake.
And if he smite out his bondman’s tooth, or his {{c1::bondwoman’s}} tooth, he shall let him go free for his tooth’s sake.
And if an ox gore a man or a woman, that they die, the ox shall be {{c1::surely}} stoned, and its flesh shall not be eaten; but the owner of the ox shall be quit.
And if an ox gore a man or a woman, that they die, the ox shall be surely {{c1::stoned}}, and its flesh shall not be eaten; but the owner of the ox shall be quit.

But if the ox was wont to gore in time past, and {{c1::warning}} hath been given to its owner, and he hath not kept it in, but it hath killed a man or a woman; the ox shall be stoned, and its owner also shall be put to death.

But if the ox was wont to gore in time past, and warning hath been given to its owner, and he hath not kept it in, but it hath {{c1::killed}} a man or a woman; the ox shall be stoned, and its owner also shall be put to death.
If there be laid on him a {{c1::ransom}}, then he shall give for the redemption of his life whatsoever is laid upon him.
If there be laid on him a ransom, then he shall give for the {{c1::redemption}} of his life whatsoever is laid upon him.
{{c1::Whether}} it have gored a son, or have gored a daughter, according to this judgment shall it be done unto him.
Whether it have gored a son, or have gored a {{c1::daughter}}, according to this judgment shall it be done unto him.
If the ox gore a {{c1::bondman}} or a bondwoman, he shall give unto their master thirty shekels of silver, and the ox shall be stoned.
If the ox gore a bondman or a {{c1::bondwoman}}, he shall give unto their master thirty shekels of silver, and the ox shall be stoned.
And if a man shall open a pit, or if a man shall dig a pit and not cover it, and an ox or an ass fall {{c1::therein}},
And if one man’s ox hurt {{c1::another’s}}, so that it dieth; then they shall sell the live ox, and divide the price of it; and the dead also they shall divide.
And if one man’s ox hurt another’s, so that it dieth; then they shall sell the live ox, and {{c1::divide}} the price of it; and the dead also they shall divide.
Or if it be known that the ox was wont to gore in time past, and its owner hath not kept it in; he shall {{c1::surely}} pay ox for ox, and the dead beast shall be his own.
If a thief be found {{c1::breaking}} in, and be smitten so that he dieth, there shall be no bloodguiltiness for him.
If a thief be found breaking in, and be {{c1::smitten}} so that he dieth, there shall be no bloodguiltiness for him.
If the sun be risen upon him, there shall be {{c1::bloodguiltiness}} for him—he shall make restitution; if he have nothing, then he shall be sold for his theft.
If the sun be risen upon him, there shall be bloodguiltiness for him—he shall make {{c1::restitution}}; if he have nothing, then he shall be sold for his theft.
If the theft be found in his hand alive, {{c1::whether}} it be ox, or ass, or sheep, he shall pay double.
If the theft be found in his hand alive, whether it be ox, or ass, or sheep, he shall pay {{c1::double}}.

If a man cause a field or {{c1::vineyard}} to be eaten, and shall let his beast loose, and it feed in another man’s field; of the best of his own field, and of the best of his own vineyard, shall he make restitution.

If a man cause a field or vineyard to be eaten, and shall let his beast loose, and it feed in {{c1::another}} man’s field; of the best of his own field, and of the best of his own vineyard, shall he make restitution.
If fire break out, and catch in {{c1::thorns}}, so that the shocks of corn, or the standing corn, or the field are consumed; he that kindled the fire shall surely make restitution.
If fire break out, and catch in thorns, so that the {{c1::shocks}} of corn, or the standing corn, or the field are consumed; he that kindled the fire shall surely make restitution.
If a man {{c1::deliver}} unto his neighbour money or stuff to keep, and it be stolen out of the man’s house; if the thief be found, he shall pay double.
If a man deliver unto his {{c1::neighbour}} money or stuff to keep, and it be stolen out of the man’s house; if the thief be found, he shall pay double.
If the thief be not found, then the {{c1::master}} of the house shall come near unto God, to see whether he have not put his hand unto his neighbour’s goods.
If the thief be not found, then the master of the house shall come near unto God, to see {{c1::whether}} he have not put his hand unto his neighbour’s goods.

For every {{c1::matter}} of trespass, whether it be for ox, for ass, for sheep, for raiment, or for any manner of lost thing, whereof one saith: 'This is it,' the cause of both parties shall come before God; he whom God shall condemn shall pay double unto his neighbour.

For every matter of {{c1::trespass}}, whether it be for ox, for ass, for sheep, for raiment, or for any manner of lost thing, whereof one saith: 'This is it,' the cause of both parties shall come before God; he whom God shall condemn shall pay double unto his neighbour.
If a man {{c1::deliver}} unto his neighbour an ass, or an ox, or a sheep, or any beast, to keep, and it die, or be hurt, or driven away, no man seeing it;
If a man deliver unto his {{c1::neighbour}} an ass, or an ox, or a sheep, or any beast, to keep, and it die, or be hurt, or driven away, no man seeing it;
the oath of the LORD shall be {{c1::between}} them both, to see whether he have not put his hand unto his neighbour’s goods; and the owner thereof shall accept it, and he shall not make restitution.
the oath of the LORD shall be between them both, to see {{c1::whether}} he have not put his hand unto his neighbour’s goods; and the owner thereof shall accept it, and he shall not make restitution.
But if it be {{c1::stolen}} from him, he shall make restitution unto the owner thereof.
But if it be stolen from him, he shall make {{c1::restitution}} unto the owner thereof.
If it be torn in {{c1::pieces}}, let him bring it for witness; he shall not make good that which was torn.
If it be torn in pieces, let him bring it for {{c1::witness}}; he shall not make good that which was torn.
And if a man {{c1::borrow}} aught of his neighbour, and it be hurt, or die, the owner thereof not being with it, he shall surely make restitution.
And if a man borrow aught of his {{c1::neighbour}}, and it be hurt, or die, the owner thereof not being with it, he shall surely make restitution.
If the owner {{c1::thereof}} be with it, he shall not make it good; if it be a hireling, he loseth his hire.
If the owner thereof be with it, he shall not make it good; if it be a {{c1::hireling}}, he loseth his hire.
And if a man {{c1::entice}} a virgin that is not betrothed, and lie with her, he shall surely pay a dowry for her to be his wife.
And if a man entice a {{c1::virgin}} that is not betrothed, and lie with her, he shall surely pay a dowry for her to be his wife.
If her {{c1::father}} utterly refuse to give her unto him, he shall pay money according to the dowry of virgins.
If her father {{c1::utterly}} refuse to give her unto him, he shall pay money according to the dowry of virgins.
Thou shalt not {{c1::suffer}} a sorceress to live.
Thou shalt not suffer a {{c1::sorceress}} to live.
{{c1::Whosoever}} lieth with a beast shall surely be put to death.
Whosoever lieth with a beast shall {{c1::surely}} be put to death.
He that {{c1::sacrificeth}} unto the gods, save unto the LORD only, shall be utterly destroyed.
He that sacrificeth unto the gods, save unto the LORD only, shall be {{c1::utterly}} destroyed.
And a stranger shalt thou not wrong, neither shalt thou oppress him; for ye were strangers in the land of {{c1::Egypt}}.
And a {{c1::stranger}} shalt thou not wrong, neither shalt thou oppress him; for ye were strangers in the land of Egypt.
Ye shall not {{c1::afflict}} any widow, or fatherless child.
Ye shall not afflict any widow, or {{c1::fatherless}} child.
If thou afflict them in any wise—for if they cry at all unto {{c1::Me}}, I will surely hear their cry—
If thou afflict them in any wise—for if they cry at all unto Me, {{c1::I}} will surely hear their cry—
My wrath shall wax hot, and {{c1::I}} will kill you with the sword; and your wives shall be widows, and your children fatherless.
My wrath shall wax hot, and I will kill you with the sword; and your wives shall be {{c1::widows}}, and your children fatherless.
If thou lend money to any of {{c1::My}} people, even to the poor with thee, thou shalt not be to him as a creditor; neither shall ye lay upon him interest.
If thou lend money to any of My {{c1::people}}, even to the poor with thee, thou shalt not be to him as a creditor; neither shall ye lay upon him interest.
If thou at all take thy {{c1::neighbour’s}} garment to pledge, thou shalt restore it unto him by that the sun goeth down;
If thou at all take thy neighbour’s {{c1::garment}} to pledge, thou shalt restore it unto him by that the sun goeth down;
for that is his only covering, it is his garment for his skin; wherein shall he sleep? and it shall come to pass, when he crieth unto {{c1::Me}}, that I will hear; for I am gracious.
for that is his only covering, it is his garment for his skin; wherein shall he sleep? and it shall come to pass, when he crieth unto Me, that {{c1::I}} will hear; for I am gracious.
Thou shalt not {{c1::revile}} God, nor curse a ruler of thy people.
Thou shalt not revile God, nor curse a ruler of thy {{c1::people}}.
Thou shalt not delay to offer of the fulness of thy harvest, and of the outflow of thy presses. The first-born of thy sons shalt thou give unto {{c1::Me}}.
Thou shalt not delay to offer of the {{c1::fulness}} of thy harvest, and of the outflow of thy presses. The first-born of thy sons shalt thou give unto Me.
Likewise shalt thou do with thine oxen, and with thy sheep; seven days it shall be with its dam; on the eighth day thou shalt give it {{c1::Me}}.
{{c1::Likewise}} shalt thou do with thine oxen, and with thy sheep; seven days it shall be with its dam; on the eighth day thou shalt give it Me.
And ye shall be holy men unto {{c1::Me}}; therefore ye shall not eat any flesh that is torn of beasts in the field; ye shall cast it to the dogs.
And ye shall be holy men unto Me; {{c1::therefore}} ye shall not eat any flesh that is torn of beasts in the field; ye shall cast it to the dogs.
Thou shalt not utter a false {{c1::report}}; put not thy hand with the wicked to be an unrighteous witness.
Thou shalt not utter a false report; put not thy hand with the {{c1::wicked}} to be an unrighteous witness.
Thou shalt not {{c1::follow}} a multitude to do evil; neither shalt thou bear witness in a cause to turn aside after a multitude to pervert justice;
Thou shalt not follow a {{c1::multitude}} to do evil; neither shalt thou bear witness in a cause to turn aside after a multitude to pervert justice;
{{c1::neither}} shalt thou favour a poor man in his cause.
neither shalt thou {{c1::favour}} a poor man in his cause.
If thou meet thine {{c1::enemy’s}} ox or his ass going astray, thou shalt surely bring it back to him again.
If thou meet thine enemy’s ox or his ass going {{c1::astray}}, thou shalt surely bring it back to him again.
If thou see the ass of him that {{c1::hateth}} thee lying under its burden, thou shalt forbear to pass by him; thou shalt surely release it with him.
If thou see the ass of him that hateth thee lying under its {{c1::burden}}, thou shalt forbear to pass by him; thou shalt surely release it with him.
Thou shalt not wrest the {{c1::judgment}} of thy poor in his cause.
Keep thee far from a false matter; and the innocent and righteous slay thou not; for {{c1::I}} will not justify the wicked.
Keep thee far from a false {{c1::matter}}; and the innocent and righteous slay thou not; for I will not justify the wicked.
And thou shalt take no gift; for a gift {{c1::blindeth}} them that have sight, and perverteth the words of the righteous.
And thou shalt take no gift; for a gift blindeth them that have sight, and {{c1::perverteth}} the words of the righteous.
And a stranger shalt thou not oppress; for ye know the heart of a stranger, seeing ye were strangers in the land of {{c1::Egypt}}.
And a {{c1::stranger}} shalt thou not oppress; for ye know the heart of a stranger, seeing ye were strangers in the land of Egypt.
And six years thou shalt sow thy land, and {{c1::gather}} in the increase thereof;
And six years thou shalt sow thy land, and gather in the {{c1::increase}} thereof;

but the {{c1::seventh}} year thou shalt let it rest and lie fallow, that the poor of thy people may eat; and what they leave the beast of the field shall eat. In like manner thou shalt deal with thy vineyard, and with thy oliveyard.

but the seventh year thou shalt let it rest and lie {{c1::fallow}}, that the poor of thy people may eat; and what they leave the beast of the field shall eat. In like manner thou shalt deal with thy vineyard, and with thy oliveyard.
Six days thou shalt do thy work, but on the {{c1::seventh}} day thou shalt rest; that thine ox and thine ass may have rest, and the son of thy handmaid, and the stranger, may be refreshed.
Six days thou shalt do thy work, but on the seventh day thou shalt rest; that thine ox and thine ass may have rest, and the son of thy {{c1::handmaid}}, and the stranger, may be refreshed.
And in all things that {{c1::I}} have said unto you take ye heed; and make no mention of the name of other gods, neither let it be heard out of thy mouth. .
And in all {{c1::things}} that I have said unto you take ye heed; and make no mention of the name of other gods, neither let it be heard out of thy mouth. .
Three times thou shalt keep a feast unto {{c1::Me}} in the year.

The feast of unleavened bread shalt thou keep; seven days thou shalt eat unleavened bread, as {{c1::I}} commanded thee, at the time appointed in the month Abib—for in it thou camest out from Egypt; and none shall appear before Me empty;

The feast of unleavened bread shalt thou keep; seven days thou shalt eat unleavened bread, as I commanded thee, at the time appointed in the month {{c1::Abib}}—for in it thou camest out from Egypt; and none shall appear before Me empty;

and the feast of {{c1::harvest}}, the first-fruits of thy labours, which thou sowest in the field; and the feast of ingathering, at the end of the year, when thou gatherest in thy labours out of the field.

and the feast of harvest, the first-{{c1::fruits}} of thy labours, which thou sowest in the field; and the feast of ingathering, at the end of the year, when thou gatherest in thy labours out of the field.
Three times in the year all thy males shall {{c1::appear}} before the Lord GOD.
Three times in the year all thy males shall appear {{c1::before}} the Lord GOD.
Thou shalt not offer the blood of {{c1::My}} sacrifice with leavened bread; neither shall the fat of My feast remain all night until the morning.
Thou shalt not offer the blood of My {{c1::sacrifice}} with leavened bread; neither shall the fat of My feast remain all night until the morning.
The choicest first-fruits of thy land thou shalt bring into the house of the LORD thy God. {{c1::Thou}} shalt not seethe a kid in its mother’s milk.
The {{c1::choicest}} first-fruits of thy land thou shalt bring into the house of the LORD thy God. Thou shalt not seethe a kid in its mother’s milk.
Behold, {{c1::I}} send an angel before thee, to keep thee by the way, and to bring thee into the place which I have prepared.
{{c1::Behold}}, I send an angel before thee, to keep thee by the way, and to bring thee into the place which I have prepared.
Take heed of him, and hearken unto his voice; be not rebellious against him; for he will not pardon your transgression; for {{c1::My}} name is in him.
Take heed of him, and {{c1::hearken}} unto his voice; be not rebellious against him; for he will not pardon your transgression; for My name is in him.
But if thou shalt indeed hearken unto his voice, and do all that {{c1::I}} speak; then I will be an enemy unto thine enemies, and an adversary unto thine adversaries.
But if thou shalt {{c1::indeed}} hearken unto his voice, and do all that I speak; then I will be an enemy unto thine enemies, and an adversary unto thine adversaries.
For {{c1::Mine}} angel shall go before thee, and bring thee in unto the Amorite, and the Hittite, and the Perizzite, and the Canaanite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite; and I will cut them off.
For Mine angel shall go before thee, and bring thee in unto the {{c1::Amorite}}, and the Hittite, and the Perizzite, and the Canaanite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite; and I will cut them off.
Thou shalt not bow down to their gods, nor serve them, nor do after their {{c1::doings}}; but thou shalt utterly overthrow them, and break in pieces their pillars.
Thou shalt not bow down to their gods, nor serve them, nor do after their doings; but thou shalt {{c1::utterly}} overthrow them, and break in pieces their pillars.
And ye shall serve the LORD your God, and {{c1::He}} will bless thy bread, and thy water; and I will take sickness away from the midst of thee.
And ye shall serve the LORD your God, and He will bless thy bread, and thy water; and {{c1::I}} will take sickness away from the midst of thee.
None shall miscarry, nor be barren, in thy land; the number of thy days {{c1::I}} will fulfil.
None shall {{c1::miscarry}}, nor be barren, in thy land; the number of thy days I will fulfil.
I will send {{c1::My}} terror before thee, and will discomfit all the people to whom thou shalt come, and I will make all thine enemies turn their backs unto thee.
I will send My {{c1::terror}} before thee, and will discomfit all the people to whom thou shalt come, and I will make all thine enemies turn their backs unto thee.
And {{c1::I}} will send the hornet before thee, which shall drive out the Hivite, the Canaanite, and the Hittite, from before thee.
And I will send the hornet before thee, which shall drive out the {{c1::Hivite}}, the Canaanite, and the Hittite, from before thee.
I will not drive them out from {{c1::before}} thee in one year, lest the land become desolate, and the beasts of the field multiply against thee.
I will not drive them out from before thee in one year, lest the land {{c1::become}} desolate, and the beasts of the field multiply against thee.
By little and little {{c1::I}} will drive them out from before thee, until thou be increased, and inherit the land.
By {{c1::little}} and little I will drive them out from before thee, until thou be increased, and inherit the land.

And {{c1::I}} will set thy border from the Red Sea even unto the sea of the Philistines, and from the wilderness unto athe River; for I will deliver the inhabitants of the land into your hand; and thou shalt drive them out before thee.

And I will set thy border from the {{c1::Red}} Sea even unto the sea of the Philistines, and from the wilderness unto athe River; for I will deliver the inhabitants of the land into your hand; and thou shalt drive them out before thee.
Thou shalt make no {{c1::covenant}} with them, nor with their gods.
They shall not dwell in thy land—lest they make thee sin against {{c1::Me}}, for thou wilt serve their gods—for they will be a snare unto thee.
They shall not dwell in thy land—lest they make thee sin {{c1::against}} Me, for thou wilt serve their gods—for they will be a snare unto thee.
And unto {{c1::Moses}} He said: ‘Come up unto the LORD, thou, and Aaron, Nadab, and Abihu, and seventy of the elders of Israel; and worship ye afar off;
And unto Moses {{c1::He}} said: ‘Come up unto the LORD, thou, and Aaron, Nadab, and Abihu, and seventy of the elders of Israel; and worship ye afar off;
and {{c1::Moses}} alone shall come near unto the LORD; but they shall not come near; neither shall the people go up with him.’
and Moses alone shall come near unto the LORD; but they shall not come near; {{c1::neither}} shall the people go up with him.’

And {{c1::Moses}} came and told the people all the words of the LORD, and all the ordinances; and all the people answered with one voice, and said: ‘All the words which the Lord hath spoken will we do.’

And Moses came and told the people all the words of the LORD, and all the ordinances; and all the people answered with one voice, and said: ‘{{c1::All}} the words which the Lord hath spoken will we do.’
And {{c1::Moses}} wrote all the words of the LORD, and rose up early in the morning, and builded an altar under the mount, and twelve pillars, according to the twelve tribes of Israel.
And Moses wrote all the words of the LORD, and rose up early in the morning, and builded an altar under the mount, and twelve pillars, according to the twelve tribes of {{c1::Israel}}.
And he sent the young men of the children of {{c1::Israel}}, who offered burnt-offerings, and sacrificed peace-offerings of oxen unto the LORD.
And he sent the young men of the {{c1::children}} of Israel, who offered burnt-offerings, and sacrificed peace-offerings of oxen unto the LORD.
And {{c1::Moses}} took half of the blood, and put it in basins; and half of the blood he dashed against the altar.
And Moses took half of the blood, and put it in {{c1::basins}}; and half of the blood he dashed against the altar.
And he took the book of the covenant, and read in the hearing of the people; and they said: ‘{{c1::All}} that the LORD hath spoken will we do, and obey.’
And he took the book of the {{c1::covenant}}, and read in the hearing of the people; and they said: ‘All that the LORD hath spoken will we do, and obey.’
And {{c1::Moses}} took the blood, and sprinkled it on the people, and said: ‘Behold the blood of the covenant, which the LORD hath made with you in agreement with all these words.’
And Moses took the blood, and sprinkled it on the people, and said: ‘{{c1::Behold}} the blood of the covenant, which the LORD hath made with you in agreement with all these words.’
Then went up {{c1::Moses}}, and Aaron, Nadab, and Abihu, and seventy of the elders of Israel;
Then went up Moses, and {{c1::Aaron}}, Nadab, and Abihu, and seventy of the elders of Israel;
and they saw the God of {{c1::Israel}}; and there was under His feet the like of a paved work of sapphire stone, and the like of the very heaven for clearness.
and they saw the God of Israel; and there was under His feet the like of a paved work of {{c1::sapphire}} stone, and the like of the very heaven for clearness.
And upon the nobles of the children of {{c1::Israel}} He laid not His hand; and they beheld God, and did eat and drink.
